---
title: "The Python engine — Jupyter under the hood"
description: "Run numpy, scipy, pandas and matplotlib at render time. The figure below is computed, not pasted."
image: thumbnail.svg
image-alt: "The Python engine — Jupyter under the hood — Blood Doctor · Quarto"
date: "2026-07-21"
categories: [Engines, Reproducibility]
---

When a document is rendered, Quarto hands every ```` ```{python} ```` block to a
Jupyter kernel, executes it, and embeds the result — a figure, a table, a value —
back into the page. Nothing is copied and pasted, so nothing can go stale.

## The idea in three lines

A Python code cell is written with a `{python}` engine tag and optional
YAML-style options after `#|`:

````markdown

::: {#cell-fig-pk .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
t = np.linspace(0, 48, 400)
C = (1000/50) * np.exp(-np.log(2)/12 * t)
plt.plot(t, C, color="#B71C1C", lw=2.2); plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![Plasma concentration after a 1000 mg IV bolus.](index_files/figure-html/fig-pk-output-1.png){#fig-pk width=584 height=411}
:::
:::


````

`#| label: fig-pk` makes the figure cross-referenceable — write `@fig-pk`
anywhere and Quarto inserts the correct number and a hyperlink.

## Why it matters clinically

Every pharmacokinetic curve, every summary table, every p-value is regenerated
from the data each time the paper is built. Re-run with new numbers and the
prose, figures and tables all move together. That is reproducibility you can
defend in front of a reviewer.

::: {.callout-tip}
## See it running live
The [**Python engine page**](/engines/python.qmd) executes this pharmacokinetic
model in full — curve, numerical AUC, and a `pandas` table — all at render time.
:::

The environment is pinned outside the synced Drive folder (`~/.venvs/quarto`) so
package installs never trigger a cloud-sync storm.
